note_text: Mr. J is a 45-year-old male presenting with substernal chest pain. He has a 20 pack-year smoking history and reports continuing to smoke daily. No prior cardiac events. Hypertension on lisinopril.
62-year-old woman with longstanding type 2 diabetes here for routine follow-up. Hba1c 8.2. She quit smoking 5 years ago after roughly 30 years of a pack a day.
8-year-old boy with recurrent abdominal discomfort over the past 3 months. No medical history. Family history unremarkable. Patient does not smoke (age noted).

78-year-old woman admitted overnight with shortness of breath and productive cough. PMH includes chronic kidney issues and well-controlled type 2 diabetes on metformin. Distant smoking history; quit decades ago.
Patient age 32, presenting with persistent headache for 2 weeks. No chronic conditions. No tobacco use ever documented in chart.
